title: Condemning the Government of Mexico for failing to fulfill its water deliveries on an annual basis to the United States under the treaty between the United States and Mexico regarding the utilization of the Colorado and Tijuana Rivers and of the Rio Grande.
plain_summary: This resolution criticizes Mexico for not keeping its promise to deliver water to the United States. In 1944, the US and Mexico signed a treaty that said they would share water from rivers that flow between the two countries, including the Colorado River and Rio Grande. Mexico was supposed to deliver a certain amount of water to the US every year, but it has been failing to do so. The House of Representatives is formally expressing its disapproval of Mexico for breaking this water-sharing agreement.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Resolution Condemning Mexico
sections.text: The House of Representatives officially criticizes the Government of Mexico for not delivering the amount of water it promised to give the United States each year. This water sharing agreement is part of a treaty (a formal agreement between countries) between the United States and Mexico about how to use water from the Colorado River, Tijuana River, and Rio Grande River.
